content:
# ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Scheme)

## What is ELSS?

ELSS stands for Equity Linked Savings Scheme. It is a type of diversified equity mutual fund that qualifies for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961.

## ELSS Lock-in Period

ELSS funds have a mandatory lock-in period of **3 years** from the date of investment. This is the shortest lock-in period among all Section 80C eligible investment options (PPF has 15 years, NSC has 5 years, Tax-saving FD has 5 years).

### Key Rules:
- The 3-year lock-in applies to each SIP installment individually. For example, if you start a monthly SIP in January 2024, the January installment unlocks in January 2027, the February installment unlocks in February 2027, and so on.
- You cannot redeem, transfer, or pledge ELSS units during the lock-in period.
- After the 3-year lock-in period, units can be redeemed at any time without any exit load.
- There is no upper limit on the investment amount, but the tax deduction under Section 80C is capped at ₹1,50,000 (₹1.5 lakh) per financial year.

## Tax Treatment of ELSS

- **Investment:** Eligible for tax deduction under Section 80C up to ₹1.5 lakh per financial year.
- **Redemption (after lock-in):** Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) exceeding ₹1.25 lakh in a financial year are taxed at 12.5%. LTCG up to ₹1.25 lakh is exempt from tax.
- **Dividends:** Taxable at the investor's income tax slab rate.

## ELSS vs Other Tax-Saving Instruments

| Feature | ELSS | PPF | NSC | Tax-saving FD |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Lock-in Period | 3 years | 15 years | 5 years | 5 years |
| Returns | Market-linked | 7.1% (current) | 7.7% (current) | 5-7% (varies) |
| Risk | High (equity) | Low (govt backed) | Low (govt backed) | Low (bank backed) |
| Section 80C Limit | ₹1.5 lakh | ₹1.5 lakh | ₹1.5 lakh | ₹1.5 lakh |

## Note
None of the 5 funds currently covered in this assistant (Bandhan Small Cap, Parag Parikh Flexi Cap, HDFC Mid-Cap Opportunities, HDFC Flexi Cap, Nippon India Large Cap) are ELSS funds. For ELSS-specific scheme details, please refer to the respective AMC website or AMFI India.

# How to Download Mutual Fund Statements and Capital Gains Reports

## Consolidated Account Statement (CAS)

A CAS is a single combined statement showing all your mutual fund holdings across all AMCs (Asset Management Companies). It is issued by the two Registrar & Transfer Agents (RTAs): **CAMS** and **KFintech**.

### How to Download CAS from CAMS
1. Visit the CAMS website: [www.camsonline.com](https://www.camsonline.com)
2. Go to **Investors** → **Statements** → **Consolidated Account Statement**.
3. Enter your registered email address and PAN.
4. Select the statement period (e.g., specific date range or financial year).
5. The CAS will be sent to your registered email as a password-protected PDF. The password is your PAN in lowercase.

### How to Download CAS from KFintech
1. Visit the KFintech website: [www.kfintech.com](https://www.kfintech.com)
2. Navigate to **Investor Services** → **Account Statement**.
3. Enter your PAN and registered email.
4. Select the desired date range.
5. The statement will be emailed to you.

### How to Download CAS from MFCentral
1. Visit [www.mfcentral.com](https://www.mfcentral.com)
2. Register or log in using your PAN and mobile number.
3. Go to **Services** → **CAS Request**.
4. Select the date range and download the statement directly.

## Capital Gains Statement

A Capital Gains Statement is essential for filing income tax returns. It details all your mutual fund redemptions, switches, and dividend income during a financial year.

### How to Download from CAMS
1. Visit [www.camsonline.com](https://www.camsonline.com)
2. Go to **Investors** → **Tax Centre** → **Capital Gains Statement**.
3. Enter your PAN and registered email.
4. Select the financial year.
5. The capital gains statement will be emailed to you.

### How to Download from KFintech
1. Visit [www.kfintech.com](https://www.kfintech.com)
2. Navigate to **Investor Services** → **Capital Gain / Loss Statement**.
3. Enter your PAN and email.
4. Select the financial year and submit.

## Important Notes
- Always use your PAN registered with the mutual fund folio.
- Statements are typically password-protected. The password is your PAN in lowercase.
- For any discrepancies, contact the respective AMC or RTA directly.
- You can also access statements through the Groww app or other platforms where your investments are linked.
